# 0011 — Cards are readable pages, not thumbnails

**Decision.** Artifact cards render markdown as genuinely readable typography — a serif reading
face at a real measure — legible at 100% zoom, before any pan or zoom is added.

**Why.** If the board isn't worth reading at rest, panning won't save it. The artifacts are
documents; typography is the design.

**In their words.**

> If I have to zoom in to read a card, you've already lost me. Make it a page I'd actually read.

**Rejected alternatives.**
- Thumbnail previews that expand on click — disqualified: it makes the board a launcher, not a
  reading surface.

**True cost.** build: medium / support: low.

**Status note.** Rests on A1 — a readable restatement only matters if reviewers trust it.
